rawContent: {"stage":"review-fix","insights":[{"kind":"gotcha","text":"When mocking `@/getPayload`, test assertions for error messages and HTTP status codes must match the actual response strings/codes in the source exactly—test failures here reveal real mismatches, not ","scope":"src/auth/withAuth.test.ts"},{"kind":"convention","text":"Auth HOC tests are split into a unit file (`src/auth/withAuth.test.ts`) and an integration file (`tests/int/auth-protected-routes.int.spec.ts`).","scope":"testing"},{"kind":"lesson","text":"Error message assertions in tests (e.g., `'No token provided'`) must exactly match the string returned by the HOC; discrepancies are caught as test failures indicating source/test drift.","scope":"testing"}]}
